_comment: Composite-type bridge for L2 plan-adherence checking. Maps a composite type name Plan might use in a [PLAN: TYPE : ...] description to its exact, real expansion in Dictum's actual grammar vocabulary (see compiler/dictumc/grammar.py's TYPE_WORDS for the ground-truth primitive set this must stay consistent with). Each entry is verified by an explicit field-name/type-count match, never a fuzzy similarity guess — if a composite type used in a plan description is not listed here, checkL2Fields() reports it as 'unverifiable' rather than attempting to guess whether the generated fields are equivalent.
_maintenance: Whenever compiler/dictumc/grammar.py's TYPE_WORDS set changes, re-check every 'fieldType' value below still names a real primitive. scripts/check_type_bridge.js (or an equivalent check added to generate_gbnf.py --check) should fail loudly if this file drifts out of sync with the real grammar, the same way GBNF staleness is already caught.

vec2.description: 2D vector — two fractional number fields, named {base}_x, {base}_y

vec3.description: 3D vector — three fractional number fields, named {base}_x, {base}_y, {base}_z

vec4.description: 4D vector / quaternion-like — four fractional number fields, named {base}_x, {base}_y, {base}_z, {base}_w

point2d.description: alias for vec2 — same expansion, different common name

point3d.description: alias for vec3 — same expansion, different common name

rgb.description: RGB color — three whole number fields, named {base}_r, {base}_g, {base}_b (0-255 range by convention, not enforced)

rgba.description: RGBA color — four whole number fields, named {base}_r, {base}_g, {base}_b, {base}_a

range.description: a min/max bound pair — two fractional number fields, named {base}_min, {base}_max

pair.description: a generic two-value pair — defaults to whole number; if Plan specifies a different element type, that type is used for both fields instead of this default
